fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity florida, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to florida, respiratory therapist compact florida, florida respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Florida Board of Respiratory Care
board.parent_dept: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Florida.

steps.title: Graduate from an Approved Respiratory Care Program
steps.description: Complete a respiratory care program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation for Respiratory Care (CoARC) or its successor.

steps.title: Pass the NBRC Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Examination.

steps.title: Apply for a Florida Respiratory Therapist License
steps.description: Submit an application, required documentation, and fees to the Florida Board of Respiratory Care.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Florida?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Florida
hero.dek: Florida Respiratory Therapist licenses require graduation from an approved respiratory care program, passing the NBRC exam, and a background check.
